synonyms: to stay for the rainy season

summary: The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words ending with 'ና' were split into their base word and the conjunction 'እና' (e.g., 'ከጋበዙኝና' -> 'ከጋበዙኝ', 'እና'). The word 'ቶዞ' was discarded as it lacked clear semantic value and could not be corrected with certainty (Protocol 2). Minor OCR errors were corrected with high certainty: 'በእኔንት' was corrected to 'በእኔነት' (Protocol 3), and 'ትገልጽላችወአን' was corrected to 'ትገልጽላችሁ' (Protocol 3), as 'ወአን' is a common OCR distortion of 'ሁ' in this context. Words like 'ማካሄጃም' and 'መዝጊያዎችም' were kept as single words, as the 'ም' is an enclitic particle rather than a separate word to be split according to Protocol 1, and their roots were extracted without the enclitic.

The batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. No words required splitting (PROTOCOL 1). Two words were discarded (PROTOCOL 2 & 3): 'አኢወማ' was deemed semantic nonsense, and 'በቆፈነነ' was an ambiguous OCR error where a certain correction could not be made. One word, 'ሀሪቷ', was corrected to 'ሀገሪቷ' (the country) based on a highly probable and common OCR error (missing 'ገ'), which is a 'Scholarly Guess' under PROTOCOL 3. All remaining words were successfully lemmatized (PROTOCOL 4), translated (PROTOCOL 5), and assigned a high-level part of speech (PROTOCOL 6). All included words met the minimum length requirement (PROTOCOL 7).

The batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. One word, 'እናውራበየት', was split into 'እናውራ' (Verb) and 'በየት' (Adverb) as per PROTOCOL 1 (THE SPLITTER) due to clear morphological boundaries. 'መልካአምድራዊ' was corrected to 'መልካምድራዊ' under PROTOCOL 3 (THE CORRECTOR) as the double 'አ' was identified as a minor OCR error in a common compound word. All other words were deemed valid and processed without splits or significant corrections. PROTOCOL 2 (THE JUDGE) did not result in any discards, as all input strings were either valid or correctable to semantically meaningful Amharic words. PROTOCOL 7 (THE FILTER) ensured no single-character words were included.

The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words required splitting: 'ያልኩትና' into 'ያልኩት' and 'እና'; 'አነሰተኛና' into 'አነስተኛ' and 'እና'; 'በኤባልና' into 'ኤባል' and 'እና'; 'ወጣገባና' into 'ወጣገባ' and 'እና'; 'እንዲያደርገኝኛ' into 'እንዲያደርገኝ' and 'እና'; and 'እንዲሰጠኝኛ' into 'እንዲሰጠኝ' and 'እና'. The suffix 'ኛ' was consistently interpreted as 'እና' (and) when attached to a preceding word. Scholarly guesses and corrections were made for common OCR errors: 'አነሰተኛ' was corrected to 'አነስተኛ' (minor spelling), 'እንሚገኙም' to 'እንደሚገኙም' (common 'ን' vs 'ድ' confusion), and 'ፀሎተኛው' to 'ጸሎተኛው' (common 'ፀ' vs 'ጸ' confusion). 'ከበለጬህ' was corrected to 'ከበለጠህ' as a plausible and semantically coherent word. 'ፍንጥቅታ' was reduced to its base noun 'ፍንጥቅ' as 'ፍንጥቅታ' is not a standard Amharic word. The string 'ጨርሰውዓም' was split, and 'ዓም' was discarded as it is not a standalone valid Amharic word and its intended meaning was ambiguous without further context, adhering to the rule of discarding meaningless combinations. All remaining words and their derived roots are at least two characters long, satisfying the size constraint. Lemmatization focused on identifying the global citation form for each word, and part-of-speech tagging was strictly limited to the eight high-level categories.

The batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. Several words ending in 'ና' were split into their base word and the conjunction 'እና' (and), such as 'ጓደኛንና' into 'ጓደኛን' and 'እና', 'የፌስቡክንና' into 'የፌስቡክን' and 'እና', 'ማስጠቃትና' into 'ማስጠቃት' and 'እና', 'ካሸነፈና' into 'ካሸነፈ' and 'እና', 'ለደንበኞቻችንና' into 'ለደንበኞቻችን' and 'እና', 'ብትጠይቅና' into 'ብትጠይቅ' and 'እና', 'አስለይቶና' into 'አስለይቶ' and 'እና', 'የቬትናምና' into 'የቬትናምን' and 'እና', and 'ሊያገኘውና' into 'ሊያገኘው' and 'እና'. The word 'ማስያዙንም' was split into 'ማስያዙን' and the adverb 'ም' (also/even). A scholarly guess was made to correct the likely OCR error 'ንድናበራ' to 'እንዲናበራ' to form a semantically valid word. Proper nouns like 'አልፓ', 'ኤልፓ', 'ሊኔ', 'ፌስቡክ', and 'ቬትናም' were identified and retained. 'በላይምቹ' was treated as a single, potentially proper, noun due to ambiguity in splitting without further context. The word 'ባፍርሳታ' was discarded as semantic nonsense, as it could not be logically corrected or identified as a valid Amharic word or root.

The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words required splitting due to merged suffixes like 'ና' (and) and 'ም' (also/even). Specifically, 'በፌደራሊስትና', 'የምትሰጥና', 'ጆቶኒውም', 'ፑሉም', and 'በዋነነትም' were split. Single-character words like 'ም' (from 'ጆቶኒውም', 'ፑሉም', 'በዋነነትም') and the meaningless 'lN' (from 'እንደlN') were discarded as per Protocol 7 and 2 respectively. Minor OCR corrections were applied to 'ከተፈጠራ' (corrected to 'ከተፈጠረ'), 'የሚያሳዬን' (corrected to 'የሚያሳየን'), 'እሴቶቻቻን' (corrected to 'እሴቶቻችን'), and 'ትውድል' (corrected to 'ትውልድ') where the context made the correction certain (Protocol 3). Scholarly guesses were made for proper nouns like 'ሲሴሮን' (Cicero) and 'ጆቶኒው' (Giotto), and the loanword 'ፑሉ' (pool). The word 'በዋነነት' was identified as an adverb with the adjective root 'ዋነኛ'. All remaining words were lemmatized, translated, and assigned a high-level part of speech.

summary: The batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. One word, 'ሊቱሊ', was discarded under PROTOCOL 2 (THE JUDGE) as it appeared to be a meaningless combination of characters or an unidentifiable foreign word, and no reliable correction could be made. A 'Scholarly Guess' was applied to 'ቅረታ', correcting it to 'ቅሬታ' under PROTOCOL 3 (THE CORRECTOR), as this is a common OCR error for a well-known Amharic word. Acronyms like 'ሲፒኤፍ' and 'ኢለርኒንግ' were identified and categorized as Nouns. All other words were successfully split, lemmatized, translated, and tagged according to the specified protocols.

summary: Processed 31 input strings. PROTOCOL 1 (THE SPLITTER) was applied to 'የሚጨፍርባቸውና', 'ሮማሪዮና', 'የኬኩና', and 'ሲገባውና', splitting each into two separate valid words (the main word and the conjunction 'ና'). For 'ባጂዮን', 'አፈጣጠርንም', 'ስምህንኮ', 'ብሰማስ', and 'ከታንዛንያም', enclitic particles ('ን', 'ንም', 'ኮ', 'ስ', 'ም') were kept attached to the 'word' field as they do not constitute standalone 'valid words' in the same manner as 'ና', but their respective roots were correctly extracted. PROTOCOL 2 (THE JUDGE) led to the discard of 'ማርጊዮልስ' as it was identified as a foreign proper noun with no discernible Amharic semantic value or correctable form. PROTOCOL 3 (THE CORRECTOR) was applied to ensure correct Amharic spelling and strip any implicit punctuation; 'ከንዐን' was accepted as a valid proper noun. PROTOCOL 4 (THE LEMMATIZER) identified the base dictionary entry (infinitive/መነሻ ቃል) for all valid words. PROTOCOL 5 (THE TRANSLATOR) provided comprehensive English synonyms. PROTOCOL 6 (THE GRAMMARIAN) assigned a high-level part of speech. PROTOCOL 7 (THE FILTER) ensured all included words and roots were at least two characters long. All 'importance' values were set to 5 as no specific ranking criteria were provided.

The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words were identified as Ge'ez (ነፍሳቲክሙ, ከመታፍቅሩ, ከልአኒክሙ, ኢትናፍቁ, በምልአ, ልብክሙ, ዘበጽድቅ, በበይናቲክሙ, ጽፉቀ) and were discarded as per PROTOCOL 2 (The Judge) because they are not modern Amharic. 'ቁዋጥርወ' and 'አተጋገል' were also discarded under PROTOCOL 2 due to being uncorrectable OCR errors or ambiguous spellings that lacked clear semantic value in Amharic without further context. PROTOCOL 1 (The Splitter) was applied to 'ተማከረችየመደብ' and 'አሏትየመደብን', splitting them into 'ተማከረች' / 'የመደብ' and 'አሏት' / 'የመደብን' respectively. PROTOCOL 3 (The Corrector) was applied to 'ከጠየቁዋቸው', correcting 'ቁዋ' to 'ቋ' to form 'ከጠየቋቸው'. For words ending with 'ና' (e.g., መደባበስና, ሳይንቅና, የምንመዝንና, ጨዋነቱንና), the 'ና' (meaning 'and') was considered part of the input string for processing, but the root was extracted without it. PROTOCOL 4 (The Lemmatizer) focused on identifying the true infinitive form, such as 'ማከማቸት' for 'ያከበተውን' to ensure the most common and representative root was used. All remaining words met PROTOCOL 7 (The Filter) by being longer than a single character.

The batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. No words required splitting (PROTOCOL 1). Two words, 'ብያለኋ' and 'በአካበባቢው', were identified as OCR errors and corrected to 'ብያለሁ' and 'በአካባቢው' respectively (PROTOCOL 3). Similarly, 'ደካም' was corrected to 'ደካማ'. 'ላውንድራስ', 'ጋርዲዮላ', and 'ቡቲኩን' were recognized as loanwords or proper nouns and their roots were kept as the words themselves, with 'ጋርዲዮላ' and 'ኮጂ' specifically identified as proper nouns (PROTOCOL 4, 5). No words were discarded as semantic nonsense (PROTOCOL 2) or for being single characters (PROTOCOL 7). All words were successfully lemmatized, translated, and assigned a high-level part of speech (PROTOCOL 4, 5, 6).
